Okay. So that is Operation Quick Start. The one thing I really want to stress for you
is that this is real. This is so real. I've lived this for over 20 years, and my business has
grown, and grown, and grown, and grown.
At the heart of it, an online business, almost all of us, are in the publishing industry
in one fashion or another. That's what I do. I'm a teacher. I'm a trainer. And foremost, I'm
a publisher. And if you look at it, the publishing industry's been around forever. I know
the Wall Street Journal has been published for ... It's coming up on 120 years. So
obviously, if that's been around for nearly 120 years, there is something there. There's
something to the publishing industry. And what we're doing online is just a newer form of
the publishing industry.
Like I said, I've been doing this for decades. Thousands of other people have online
businesses and have huge success. So this is real. You can do it. You absolutely can do it.
It's not magic. And going back to that first piece of the mental game that I talked about,
it's deciding that you're going to do it, deciding you're going to be successful, deciding
that you're going to take action, deciding that if that first action doesn't work, then you're
going to take a different action. You're going to find a way to make it work. You're not
going to try to reinvent the wheel. You're just going to buckle down and get to work.
This can work for you. Remember it's about baby steps. It's about taking those first
small steps. It's about focusing on what your next step is, not what the hundredth step is.
What's the next step? From where you are right now, what's the first step you need to
take? And then take it. And then what's the next step after that? Not what’s step 101 or
204, it's just the next step. Keep moving forward with baby steps.
You need to decide on a market. Now the market that you decide on doesn't have to
be the ultimate market. It doesn't have to be the be-all and end-all, the market that you're
going to be in forever. Look at me. I started in the stock market niche. I'm now teaching
entrepreneurs. It was a major leap pivot, a huge pivot, a complete hundred percent start
over from scratch.
The part I didn't tell you was that one of the reasons that I moved in businesses is
because my partner in my first business stole the business. So I had to start over from
scratch. But because I had been through it once before, I was able to start literally 10
times faster, and build a business that is now, I don't know, 50 times bigger, 50 times

bigger, I think. I think I'm getting the math right.
So decide on a market, but it doesn't have to be the ultimate where you stay forever.
Almost no one stays in one market forever. But decide on that market, and start building
your presence. Get out there. Start to interact with people in your market. Engage with
them. Find out what they want. Find out what their hot points are. Find out what's
holding them back, what their dreams are, what their aspirations are. Engage with them.
Have a conversation with them. You just need to get started. You need to start rubbing
elbows with the people in your market.
Then you start publishing. As you publish, well, you keep on publishing. You keep
on getting better with your message, and you sharpen your message. If you look at what
I'm publishing now compared to what I was publishing four or five years ago, compared
to what I was publishing 10 years ago, compared to what I was publishing 20 years ago,
I'm better in every way. I'm more on point. I'm more compelling, at least I think I am. I
can sell better. I can serve better. So over time, you will sharpen your message. You will
get better at delivering your message. Your message will pivot. You'll get more in
alignment with the people in your market.
Build your list. Always, always focus on building your presence,
building your community, building your email list. Everything you ... If you
want something to think about every morning when you wake up, think about how you're
going to build your list that day. That's what I did. That's why I was able to build a
business because I focused on that list. I knew every subscriber I got had value, had value
in terms of my momentum, had value in terms of building a business, and had dollar
value. So start building your list.
Work on that process. Try to get better and better lead magnets, better and better
opt-in pages, higher conversion rates, higher ... When I say that, I mean conversion from
a visitor to an opt-in. If you want to obsess about something, obsess about building your
list. If something doesn't work, then pivot. If people aren't opting in for your lead magnet,
create a different lead magnet. If people are not buying your products, change your offer,
or change your messaging around the offer.
Just decide, though, that you're going to succeed. Decide that if it doesn't work out
first, you're going to pivot. You might have to pivot your lead magnet. You might have to
pivot your offer. You might have to pivot your market. You might have to pivot your
pricing. You might have to pivot your business model. But just keep on pivoting until you
land on the one that works, and then make it better.

And then the number one thing, I just talked about it in the last section, is you need
to stay on top of your mental game. You have to stay on top of your mental game.
You have to stay at a mental peak, or if not a peak, you have to avoid the dips. You have to
forgive yourself when you do have those dips because everyone does. But it's your mental
game that's going to keep you ... that's going to pull you through, and that's going to give
you the stamina, the marketing stamina and the business stamina.
So that's Operation Quick Start. I know I covered a lot of ground, and I covered it
quickly, and sometimes I didn't go super, super deep on some things, but I gave you the
plan. This is the plan that you need to follow that will work for you. This will absolutely ...
I've seen it work for so many people, and it's going to work for you. This business is real.
You just have to get started. You have to take the next step.
Good luck.
